title: 帷幕
summary:
Following a tense argument with his daughter Judith, who rebukes him for interfering in her personal life and her friendship with Colonel Alton, Captain Hastings is approached by Nurse Crawford (also referred to as Nurse Cavell and Nurse Craven). The nurse expresses her disdain for Mrs. Franklin, claiming the woman is unintelligent and manipulative, suggesting she feigns illness and weakness to control her husband, Dr. Franklin. The conversation shifts to the heavy atmosphere of the location, which the nurse attributes to a past murder involving an old lady. Hastings reflects on whether a premeditated murder can leave a lasting psychological mark on a place.

Boyd Carrington, a man of strong and rational personality, joins the group and engages in lighthearted banter with the nurse regarding Judith’s work in the laboratory. After the nurse departs, Carrington invites Hastings to visit Nayton. Hastings consults with Poirot, who encourages the excursion, noting that Carrington is Hastings's "type."

During the trip to the grand estate of Nayton, Hastings enjoys the company of Carrington, who shares anecdotes from his time as a magistrate in India and his experiences in East Africa. The conversation turns to Poirot; Carrington expresses deep respect for Poirot’s character and professional achievements. Despite Poirot's physical decline, Carrington maintains that his mental faculties remain sharp, asserting that physical incapacity does not necessitate mental decline.
